patches.hypothesis: The original patch incorrectly truncates the string to 252 characters and appends '...' which does not generalize to all cases. The refined fix will correctly truncate the string to 255 characters and append '...' only if the original string exceeds 255 characters. Additionally, it will handle null inputs gracefully.
patches.original_hypothesis: The bug is that the sanitize method does not truncate the string to 255 characters as required. The method should append characters until it reaches 255 characters, then append '...' if the original string was longer.

patches.hypothesis: The original patch truncated the string to 252 characters and appended '...' which did not meet the requirement of exactly 255 characters. The refined patch will ensure the string is always exactly 255 characters long by truncating at 252 characters and appending '...' if necessary, using a constant for the maximum length to avoid hardcoding values.
patches.original_hypothesis: The bug is that the sanitize method does not truncate the string to 255 characters as required. The method needs to check the length of the StringBuilder and stop appending characters once it reaches 255 characters, appending '...' if the original string was longer.
